answer:
<briefing_summary>
This response provides two precisely engineered retrieval queries designed for extra-high effort agentic search within a primary-source Islamic corpus [1, 2]. The queries are structured to satisfy the specific MILOS subtask by focusing on the literal demands of the Shahada on daily life as evidenced in scriptural texts [3, 4].
</briefing_summary>

<technical_details>
To maximize retrieval recall and precision, these queries utilize classical terminology and specific source indices (Ayat/Hadith) to anchor the retrieval system in primary data rather than secondary commentary [5, 6]. The phrasing is calibrated to trigger adaptive thinking and grounded extraction, ensuring the results are direct and relevant to the "Tawhid" and "Shahada" pillars [7, 8].
</technical_details>

<queries>
1. Retrieve primary Quranic verses, specifically 2:165, 9:24, 98:5, and 65:3, that define the manifestation of Tawhid through daily reliance, absolute fear, and prioritized love for Allah.
2. Extract Sahih hadith narrations, including the "sweetness of faith" (Bukhari #16) and "branches of faith" (Bukhari #9), that specify the daily ethical and spiritual requirements for fulfilling the Shahada testimony.
</queries>

<action_items>
1. Paste the provided queries into the retrieval interface of the specified /notebooklm corpus to generate the grounding evidence [2, 9].
2. Verify that the retrieved snippets are direct primary sources to maintain the high bar for MILOS task validation [6, 10].
3. Apply the 3-tier grading system (Bayinnah/Qarina/Niyyah) to each result as previously instructed [3, 4].
</action_items>
